algorithms.name: notify documents of a tool change
algorithms.html: To notify documents of a tool change given a Document tool owner and a list of origins exposed origins, run these steps:

steps.html: Assert: these steps are running in parallel.
Let navigablesToNotify be tool owner’s node navigable’s traversable navigable’s descendant navigables.
For each navigable of navigablesToNotify:
Let targetDocument be navigable’s active document.
If targetDocument is not allowed to use the "tools" feature, then continue.

If tool is exposed to an origin given tool owner’s origin, exposed origins, and targetDocument’s origin, then queue a global task on the webmcp task source given targetDocument’s relevant global object to fire an event named toolchange at targetDocument’s associated ModelContext.

algorithms.name: tool is exposed to an origin
algorithms.html: To determine if a tool is exposed to an origin given an origin tool owner origin, a list of origins exposed origins, and an origin accessing origin, run these steps:

steps.html: If tool owner origin is same origin with accessing origin, then return true.
For each allowed origin of exposed origins:
If accessing origin is same origin with allowed origin, then return true.
Return false.

algorithms.name: model context/unregister a tool
algorithms.html: To unregister a tool given a ModelContext modelContext and a string tool name, run these steps:

steps.html: Assert: these steps are running on modelContext’s relevant agent’s event loop.
Let tool map be modelContext’s internal context’s tool map.
If tool map[tool name] does not exist, then return.
Let exposed origins be tool map[tool name]'s exposed origins.
Remove tool map[tool name].
Run the following steps in parallel:
ignored: Notify documents of a tool change given modelContext’s relevant global object’s associated Document and exposed origins.

algorithms.name: Document/modelContext
algorithms.html: The modelContext getter steps are:

steps.html: Return this’s associated ModelContext object.

algorithms.name: ModelContext/registerTool(tool, options)
algorithms.html: The registerTool(tool, options) method steps are:

steps.html: Let global be this’s relevant global object.
Let tool owner be global’s associated Document.
If tool owner is not fully active, then return a promise rejected with an "InvalidStateError" DOMException.

If this’s surrounding agent’s agent cluster’s is origin-keyed is false and this’s relevant settings object’s origin’s scheme is not "file", then return a promise rejected with a "SecurityError" DOMException.
If tool owner is not allowed to use the "tools" feature, then return a promise rejected with a "NotAllowedError" DOMException.
Let tool map be this’s internal context’s tool map.
Let tool name be tool’s name.
Let tool title be tool’s title.
If tool map[tool name] exists, then return a promise rejected with an InvalidStateError DOMException.
If tool name or description is an empty string, then return a promise rejected with an InvalidStateError DOMException.

If either tool name is the empty string, or its length is greater than 128, or if tool name contains a code point that is not an ASCII alphanumeric, U+005F (_), U+002D (-), or U+002E (.), then return a promise rejected with an InvalidStateError DOMException.
Let stringified input schema be the empty string.

If tool’s inputSchema exists, then set stringified input schema to the result of serializing a JavaScript value to a JSON string, given tool’s inputSchema. If this threw an exception, then return a promise rejected with that exception.
Let promise be a new promise created in this’s relevant realm.
Let signal be options’s signal.
If signal exists, then:
If signal is aborted, then return a promise rejected with signal’s abort reason.
Add the following abort steps to signal:
Unregister a tool given this and tool name.
Reject promise with signal’s abort reason.
Let exposed origins be an empty list of origins.
If options’s exposedTo exists, then:
For each origin of options’s exposedTo:
Let parsedURL be the result of running the URL parser on origin.
If parsedURL is failure or its origin is not potentially trustworthy, then return a promise rejected with a "SecurityError" DOMException.
Append parsedURL’s origin to exposed origins.

Let tool definition be a new tool definition, with the following items:

name

tool name

title

tool title

description

tool’s description

input schema

stringified input schema

execute steps

steps that invoke tool’s execute

annotations

null if tool’s annotations does not exist. Otherwise, an annotations with the following items:

read-only hint

tool’s annotations’s readOnlyHint

untrusted content hint

tool’s annotations’s untrustedContentHint

exposed origins

exposed origins
Set this’s internal context[tool name] to tool definition.
Run the following steps in parallel:
Notify documents of a tool change given tool owner and exposed origins.
Queue a global task on the webmcp task source given global to resolve promise with undefined.
Return promise

algorithms.name: ModelContext/getTools(options)
algorithms.html: The getTools(options) method steps are:

steps.html: Let global be this’s relevant global object.
Let toolRequestor be global’s associated Document.
If toolRequestor is not fully active, then return a promise rejected with an "InvalidStateError" DOMException.

If this’s surrounding agent’s agent cluster’s is origin-keyed is false and this’s relevant settings object’s origin’s scheme is not "file", then return a promise rejected with a "SecurityError" DOMException.
If toolRequestor is not allowed to use the "tools" feature, then return a promise rejected with a "NotAllowedError" DOMException.
Let from origins be an empty list of origins.
If options’s fromOrigins exists, then:
For each origin of options’s fromOrigins:
Let parsedURL be the result of running the URL parser on origin.
If parsedURL is failure or its origin is not potentially trustworthy, then return a promise rejected with a "SecurityError" DOMException.
Append parsedURL’s origin to from origins.
Let promise be a new promise created in this’s relevant realm.
Run the following steps in parallel:
Let tools be an empty list of RegisteredTool dictionaries.
Let navigables be toolRequestor’s node navigable’s traversable navigable’s inclusive descendant navigables.
For each navigable of navigables:
Let targetDocument be navigable’s active document.
If targetDocument is not allowed to use the "tools" feature, then continue.
Let targetOrigin be targetDocument’s origin.
Let callerOrigin be toolRequestor’s origin.
If toolOwnerIsRequested be true if targetOrigin is same origin with callerOrigin, or if from origins contains targetOrigin; otherwise, false.
If toolOwnerIsRequested is false, then continue.
Let targetToolMap be targetDocument’s associated ModelContext’s internal context’s tool map.
For each tool name → tool definition of targetToolMap:
If tool is exposed to an origin given targetOrigin, tool definition’s exposed origins, and callerOrigin returns false, then continue.

Let registeredTool be a new RegisteredTool dictionary, with the following fields:

name

tool definition’s name

title

tool definition’s title if it is non-null; otherwise the empty string.

description

tool definition’s description

inputSchema

tool definition’s input schema if it is not the empty string; otherwise undefined.

window

targetDocument’s relevant global object

origin

targetOrigin, serialized.

annotations

if tool definition’s annotations is not null, a ToolAnnotations dictionary whose readOnlyHint is tool definition’s annotations’s read-only hint and untrustedContentHint is tool definition’s annotations’s untrusted content hint.
Append registeredTool to tools.
Sort in ascending order tools, with a being less than b if a["name"] is code unit less than b["name"].
Queue a global task on the webmcp task source given global to resolve promise with tools.
Return promise.

algorithms.name: synthesize a declarative JSON Schema object algorithm
algorithms.html: The synthesize a declarative JSON Schema object algorithm, given a form element form, runs the following steps. They return a map representing a JSON Schema object. [JSON-SCHEMA]

steps.html: TODO: Derive a conformant JSON Schema object from form and its form-associated elements.

algorithms.name: perform an observation
algorithms.html: To perform an observation given a top-level traversable traversable, run these steps:

steps.html: Assert: This algorithm is running in the browser agent’s AI agent queue.
Assert: traversable’s active document is not fully active.
Let observation be a new observation.
Let flat descendants be the inclusive descendant navigables of traversable’s active document.
For each navigable descendant of flat descendants:
Let document be descendant’s active document’s.
Let id be document’s unique ID.
Set observation’s tool map[id] = document’s associated ModelContext’s internal context’s tool map’s values, which are tool definitions.

Perform any implementation-defined steps to add anything to observation that the user agent might deem useful or necessary, besides just populating the tool map. This might include annotated screenshots of the page, parts of the accessibility tree, etc.

Perform any implementation-defined steps with observation and the browser agent, to expose the observation’s tool map to the browser agent in whatever way it accepts.

Implementations are expected to convey to the browser agent any relevant security information associated with tool definitions, such as the originating origin, among other things, so that the backing model has an idea of the different parties at play, and can most safely carry out the end user’s intent.
